description: Notarize-only record of the inbound trigger that put a content item into the moderation queue: a DSA Art.16 notice, a trusted-flagger report (Art.22), an automated-detection hit, or own-initiative review. AGLedger does NOT decide anything here — this attests, signed and hash-chained, WHAT was flagged, by WHOM/what, and WHEN the clock started. Fields mirror the inputs the platform must later reflect in its DSA statement of reasons. `automatedDetection` records whether the content was DETECTED by automated means (DSA automated_detection) — distinct from whether the DECISION is automated (that lives on enforcement-decision-v1). `statementCategory` carries the real DSA STATEMENT_CATEGORY the flag asserts (named statementCategory, not category — `category` is a reserved record-metadata field). Pure notarization: reaches terminal RECORDED in one round-trip.

contentRef.description: Platform's stable identifier for the flagged content item or account (the moderation subject). Opaque to AGLedger.

contentType.description: DSA content_type — one or more media types of the flagged content. SYNTHETIC_MEDIA covers AI-generated / deepfake content.

sourceType.description: DSA source_type — how the platform learned of the content. ARTICLE_16_NOTICE: a user notice-and-action submission. TRUSTED_FLAGGER: an Art.22 trusted flagger (notices given priority). VOLUNTARY_OWN_INITIATIVE: the platform's own proactive detection/review.

automatedDetection.description: DSA automated_detection — whether the content was DETECTED by automated means (e.g. a classifier surfaced it). Distinct from automatedDecision (whether the enforcement DECISION was automated).

statementCategory.description: DSA STATEMENT_CATEGORY (14-value enum) the flag asserts as the type of illegality/incompatibility. The platform's mapping of the notice to a category is itself an attestable choice and may be revised at the enforcement decision.

submitterType.description: Optional: the kind of party who submitted the notice (for Art.16 notices). authority = a government/court order channel (Art.9/10) routed to moderation.

noticeRef.description: Optional: platform reference to the full notice text/payload held off-chain (a URL or document id). The notarized record binds the metadata; the notice body itself need not be on-chain.

contentLanguage.description: Optional ISO 639-1 language code of the content (DSA content_language).

noticeReceivedAt.description: Optional: when the notice was received / the content was detected — the start of the response clock.
